review_title: Awesome WordPress Alternative
description: I began using WebWave a few months ago and am so impressed that I believe it merits a review. It's incredibly simple to use, and I was able to quickly create a prototype of my WordPress website. WebWave is by far the simplest and quickest way I've seen to build a website. The builder interface is superior to that of the most popular builders, such as Wix and Weebly. The drag-and-drop interface allows you to edit your site down to the smallest detail before publishing it with a few mouse clicks. The design customization options are limitless, allowing me to make my site look exactly how I want it to.
author: Sunil P.
pros: All-in-one solutionEmail hosting is included, as is a free SSL certificate.E-commerce and blog featuresSeveral free pre-made templatesIt feels more like using Photoshop or Canva than website design software.I became acquainted in less than two hours due to the short learning curve.
cons: The domain renewal fee could be reduced.Nothing for the time being, but I'm sure more features will be added in the future.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Bye bye Wix/Joomla/Wordpress - hello WebWave!
description: Must have if you:+ run agency that needs a micropages (websites) for your customers products AND need a solution that will bill your customers automagically + need a simple website (doesn't mean ugly or lack of functions) with ecommerce store+ need a replacement for Wix (too expensive with more options) or Wordpress/Joomla (too complicated) that is ready to run and well optimized (Google loves it!)
author: Rafa¿ M.
pros: + hosting, email (mailbox) handling and auto-update included+ great SEO support for native templates+ real WYSIWYG+ easy to deploy, easy to use for end-user (CMS and e-commerce management)+ (agency feautre) easy way to bill your customers+ great support (docs and human assist)
cons: - more templates (grouped by business type)- more ecommerce features (I miss subcscription for physical products)- more native integrations (not thru API/Zapier but native - with email marketing systems)
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: Good for online stores
description: I use my site as a landing page for my freelancing business. Very good in terms of design as I can make it in any way I like.
author: Anamaria D.
pros: I loved the fact that you can fully build your online store from 0 to 100. No set styles, you can literally do it as you please and turn your vision into reality.
cons: The only thing I don't like at WebWave is the fact that websites are not automatically mobile optimized so you have to go in and edit every page in a way that it will look good on mobile. Takes a bit more time and makes things a bit more difficult.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: All Websites should be like this in this day and age!
description: True drag and drop website builder with modern , well thought out solutions to problems other sites have and love that though it looks like a expert builder it's still got awesom onboarding videos that makes an anxious person like me to learn new apps, really excited to use it. I get all the awesome features and well looked after without the overwhelm.
author: Inez G.
pros: 5* it's true dag and drop 5* builder layout is well thought out and easy enough 5* love the fact it's has grouping so on different views it stays put then having to manually rearrange. Shows that the team have really looked at all the problems that exist compared to its other competitors 5* the animations they are modern not that dated kind. Wow! Some of the animations with static images you would think u can only get if u created and uploaded a Giff but not on here! 5* page alters to monitor size and still places things in proportion without looking odd. Very intuitive! 5* lovely/ perfect onboarding videos that shows all the awesome features with a demo walk throughlittle under 4 mins! Perfect Demo to get u exited and get right in and testing 5* the online store building features does physical and digital products and it's very very easy in terms of steps. U don't have to navigate far at all. U can look like a wizard everything is just there Every new page /blog u create u can create and as u do it simply has u secelct where it shows , so it not something u have to go back and set up, thus keeping u very tidy and organised so u don't have broken links or missing pages. Very well thought out. I think I love the team behind this. They have a brain!
cons: I really can't think of anything to fault... It's definitely the best one I've seen to date!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: It's much better than I thought!
description: I offered a new web design for my friend's veterinary clinic business, and I had a couple of web design tool options to go with. One option was Wordpress (which used to be very Google-friendly some years ago but now I hear that it's not very good for Google), and the other was the drag-and-drop design tool called Mobirise (a great tool but very repetitive design-wise). I was aware of the Webwave but never gave it a chance to try before. And it was a good opportunity to give it a run. So I started working on this new veterinary clinic website project and learned a lot about what Webwave offers while working. Now I'm considering using Webwave only for all my future projects.
author: Bugra B.
pros: If you know, the basics of web designing or familiar to drag and drop site-builders Webwave helps you to understand things better. It functions more like UI design tools like Figma but focuses on web designing. It gives you great flexibility in creativity. And how they thought about many details in the agency panel is impressive.
cons: So far, I can't think of any solid con to consider something I dislike about this service.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A simple yet powerful web builder
description: I am trying out WebWave to build websites and landing pages for myself and my clients. So far it has been easy to use. Once I get the hang of it, I can build a website from the scratch quite quickly. It saves me time and effort.
author: Junwen C.
pros: Pros: - It is simple to use - Just need to drag and drop the elements, according to my liking - There are tutorials to guide me along - So I know exactly what the different functions do - There are many features - With the different feasures available, it can suit my needs and wants - So far it is intuitive and responsive - Can be customizable and adjustable - There are multiple templates available - So I do not need to start from scratch - It has Unsplash integration in it - Which makes finding pictures for the website much easier
cons: Cons: - It can be a bit laggy at times when editing - I will prefer it if it can be smooth - It can be a bit overwhelming with all the features - It takes a steep learning curve to understand and apply everything - The interface could be better - At the start, I was a bit confused about what to do, where the menu is, and where the different pages are
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Best White Label Website Development Platform for Non Coders
description: Webwave is the best tool now in the market compared to its alternatives.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Very easy to use drag and drop features, manage multiple client sites in one workspace, get SSL, domain, SEO analytics and business emails in just click of a button, buitl-in templates ready for use, deploy blog and ecommerce easily.
cons: Ai content creator mising,will addon shortly I believe, as well as more plugins for web portals to be added in to make ease of deployment to clients.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A great Webflow alternative for a fraction of the price
author: Marco C.
pros: Webwave is a lightweight, powerful and modular web development platform that helps developers build better websites more quickly. Webwave provides convenient features for managing files, creating pages and forms, tracking updates to pages and sites, as well as integrating with other tools. It has a clean interface, it is pretty fast and scalable. It can be white-labeled, so it's a perfect solution for agencies!
cons: It's not easy to find a developer expert in the Wewave platform, so you have to be careful about the project you'll decide to develop with it, since it may be difficult to ask for help.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: WebWave Is an Excellent Web Development System
author: Felix R.
pros: Web Wave is a great builder to make perfect custom websites. It's easy to use and the system resembles Photophop or Figma in the way it works. You can build your websites from scratch, or use the inbuilt templates. The whole system is setup in a way which can save you time.
cons: It's not cheap, but that's normal when it comes to this type of software I think. Other alternatives are quite expensive. But then again this service comes with hosting.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Excellent Website Builder
description: As a website builder, WebWave is easily the most intuitive platform I've used. Given that it works in a similar fashion to the layer-based systems in Photoshop, Figma and Affinity Designer, WebWave feels like a natural extension to the design tools that I'm already familiar with, therefore eliminating the hassles experienced with grid-based builders, such as Webflow and SiteJet etc. This has allowed me to realise my creative vision for my website much more quickly and efficiently than some of the aforementioned builders I've explored in the past. Truthfully, I've never found it easier to translate my initial ideas into a functioning and responsive website.
author: Joshua T.
pros: WebWave has many incredible features that make it a pleasure to design, publish and manage a website. The website builder is one of the most intuitive that I've used. It's simple to understand and is packed with numerous features that make designing responsive websites a breeze. WebWave also comes with a host of other features that are essential in running an online business. Below are some of my favourites:1. It has the ability to create landing pages, web-pages, pop-ups and forms2. It has animation capabilities for interactive web elements.3. WebWave gives you the ability to create and share blog posts.4. It has business features, such as an online store, multiple payment gateways and email.5. It has an excellent system for improving your website's SEO.
cons: I don't really have any features that I actively dislike. I would like WebWave to keep innovating and to bring more advancements to their animation features so that our websites can become increasingly more interactive. I think they could also add more modern template modules for us to insert into our website designs.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The tool that bridges the design gap in site builder space
description: Super flexible and fluid design that ensures customer delight as the canvas is not bound with restrictions on where and how to place elements, Super happy with the purchase.
author: Kris R.
pros: Super fluid layout, flexible element placement, design principles allowed, similarity to design tools with a web interface.
cons: Some video tutorials would help get to terms with all features that the tool has to offer.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: WebWave for Web Designers
author: Jinggo A.
pros: It is well suited for designers who'd rather focus on aesthetic and functionality rather than coding. It works like a typical graphic design software but the output is an advanced website that requires no programming background. You have full control and the freehand to make it work and look like in different screens up to the smallest details. As a designer, I hate templates and I truly don't need one when you can design all you want with the available tools and features. I finally can design a blog and forget about Wordpress. It works as a web app as well which is not possible in other platforms.
cons: It is still a work in progress. Email integration is limited and not available to major platforms, not much options to design a complex menu, shadows not possible in text, no data integration to enable a more advanced website that requires some logic, and so on. It has become expensive and more than tripled its price since I started using it. It is generally limited to simple websites, at least for now. However, I believe in the team behind this software and in their vision for this product. It just keeps on improving and for sure it will be a much better software down the road.
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: A great alternative to WIX & Webflow.
author: Steven A.
pros: I like the price the most when it comes to these types of service. Even if the price can be low on other web builders, it does add up over time.
cons: The speed time is not as great compared to other web builders, but it does the job.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good Solution to Making Your Own Website
description: I had a positive experience with WebWave. It helped me make a great looking website. It had a lot of options which I liked, and the ability to edit everything on one page easily was a real bonus.
author: Sam E.
pros: WebWave fills a gap for people that want to make a good looking website. But don't want or need to know how to set up a website from scratch. With great looking templates, and editing page that is familiar to anyone that has used a desktop product before. WebWave gives you all the options you need.
cons: I'm not sure what is missing from this to be honest. I think it has a lot of options. I do feel that they could be overwhelming for some people. So a basic option button would be great for some people.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Solid Website Builder
author: Calvin S.
pros: I have been following Webwave for over a year now and they have had regular updates. Customer support is fast at replying to my questions. I have some clients who are looking to put their business online. Webwave, with their agency features, is a great choice for me. I can white label their web builder with my own CNAME and logo. Very professional!
cons: I still needed to view their help articles to get going, hopefully in the future they can make the interface a bit more intuitive. Also, the load time in the builder can be slow.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: A fast and responsive web builder
author: Luke R.
pros: The speed of the platform is really impressive. I love how easy it is to set up and use, while still offering ample customization. The team at Webwave really did well with creating this system.
cons: A lack of a portal for client uploads. CMS is a little lacking with only a blog.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Very good website builder for professional web designers
description: I'm creating websites for my customers. I used to work with a developer to code my designs. It took a long time for him to create a working website from my design. It was also very expensive because developers are very expensive. With WebWave in most of my projects, I don't need a developer anymore. I can create a website design just as I used to do and it gets coded by WebWave. It's much faster and cheaper this way.
author: Amy R.
pros: You can create a website from scratch If you pick a template you can easily change everything just as you need White label CMS You can add your own HTML, CSS or JS code to your website Great support Great value for money ratio
cons: If you want to create an online store there is no built-in support for that. Instead, you have to pick an outside tool. Fortunately, it integrates very easily.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: I love Webwave
author: Constantin U.
pros: Pros: Everything is great, very easy to set up, and intuitive.
cons: Cons: I don't have any cons yet. I still learning about it.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Huge potential for future
author: Rezaul A.
pros: Really like the drag and drop feature overall. Has huge potential.
cons: The builder takes a bit more time to load now. This can be easily fixed. i reckon nothing to do with software in general.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: If you are a graphic designer this is the webbuilder for you
description: Easy to use. I little bit of a learning curve but could be well worth it.
author: Khushna S.
pros: Easy to use drop and drag website builder / host
cons: Mobile sites are not auto optimized and site speed isn't fast globally. Website language is Polish.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Product for total Rookie
author: Katarzyna G.
pros: The product allows beginners to create a nice website. You don't have to be an IT specialist to take advantage of many options.
cons: Although the website created by me is perfect sometimes errors appear after its publication. I had to solve some problems by myself. The help desk couldn't do it or it took too long.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Great website builder
description: Customer support is great, answers quickly and overall builder is easy to use and has got almost unlimited possibilities to make a website unique.
author: Dariusz K.
pros: It has got many useful add-ons, multiple options to change and overall it's great tool to build own website.
cons: I know that's work in progress but missing possibility to edit website from mobile phone.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Good for All
author: Antonio C.
pros: the service is very simple to use. moreover, the editor is very functional. the ability to have users is useful for giving a site to anyone and managing it
cons: When you switch from desktop and mobile more of setting are missing
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Intuitive Website builder
author: Kumar G.
pros: The distinguishing characteristic is how all the components are arranged in a layers panel similar to Photoshop so that you may layer and group them. Each piece is largely under your control, and you can change the design down to the last pixel. There are breakpoints for each screen size so that you can personalize the arrangement. At every screen size, the goal is to have total control over your layout in a fixed location.
cons: The UI of the admin dashboard can be improved for a better user experience.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use and Fast Website Builder
description: Easy to use, user-friendly, and has lots of functionalities.
author: Afroz A.
pros: No training is required to start building a website, it is easy and user-friendly.
cons: I didn't find any local SEO options in the builder.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
